summary: Executed backend-only regression and feature tests for WhatsApp webhook invoice parsing and fallback flows using public URL. All 5 implemented tests passed.

minor.issue: Regex-based payer extraction is overly greedy for mixed text payloads (captures date/GST fragments in payer in some messages).

action_items: Refine parse_transaction_text payer regex to avoid over-capturing adjacent fields (date/GSTIN).
Retest Unsiloed success-path extraction with a valid UNSILOED_KEY (currently placeholder/empty).
critical_code_review_comments: UNSILOED_KEY placeholder is present in backend/.env and fallback path works; however, production readiness depends on validating live Unsiloed schema/response mapping.
Webhook behavior currently degrades gracefully when Unsiloed unavailable, preserving endpoint stability.
seed_data_creation: Created TEST_ prefixed status records via /api/status during tests.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Public endpoint base URL sourced from /app/frontend/.env. Verified: /api/, /api/status POST+GET persistence, /api/webhook/whatsapp text-only amount+gst, media fallback with NumMedia>0 when UNSILOED_KEY empty, and graceful no-amount case.
rca of the issue: Payer extraction issue stems from broad regex in parse_transaction_text: (?i)(?:payer|from)\s*[:\-]?\s*([a-zA-Z0-9 .&_-]{2,50}). This character class allows digits/hyphens and consumes subsequent date/GST tokens until length limit. Mitigation: stop capture at known separators/keywords (on/date/gstin) or parse using token boundaries.
